rulings.Case Title: ALISA BAKER VS. EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MANAGEMENT, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 25, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF ALISA BAKER MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is CONTINUED to April 30, 2025 at 9:30 a.m., on the Court's own motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALISON THOMAS VS. ERNEST ANTONIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Relief From Default And Extension Of Time To File Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 25, 2025 line 2. PLAINTIFF ALISON THOMAS PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR RELIEF FROM DEFAULT AND EXTENSION OF TIME TO FILE AMENDED COMPLAINT is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff's request for relief is premature because there is no default or dismissal entered. Additionally, Plaintiff's requested deadline to amend the Complaint is April 5 - a date that has already passed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default; Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 25, 2025 line 3. DEFENDANT DESHAWN DAVIS , AN INDIVIDUAL, BETTY PITTMAN NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS & AUTHORITIES HEARING REQUIRED. Plaintiff, Defendants, Mr. Blama, and Ms. Butler to testify as to (1) whether there are separate units and/or entrances at the residence, (2) whether Ms. Pittman was home on May 7, 2024 and interacted with the process server, (3) whether Mr. Davis interacted with the process server on June 17, 2024, and (4) what the ABC Legal Investigation revealed regarding the process server's attempted service. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: JDS MONTGOMERY LLC ET AL VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 25, 2025 line 5. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, SAN FRANCISCO ASSESSOR-RECORDER, SAN FRANCISCO CONTROLLER, SAN FRANCISCO TAX COLLECTOR DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Complaint is SUSTAINED with leave to amend within 10 days of notice of entry of order. The intangible value of being close to certain neighbors is taxable under state property tax law, and under 1114(a) of the ordinance, the tax must be assessed on the $38 million in consideration paid because the consideration was a definite amount. Further, the ambiguity canon does not apply because the statute is unambiguous, and since the consideration paid is a definite amount of $38 million, the Court need not consider Plaintiff's cited provisions regarding fair market value. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
